title: AI can plan and complete long, multi-step work
description: An AI system can break down complex goals, track dependencies over time, and carry work through changing conditions with limited supervision.

subQuestions.title: Can it decompose goals into workable phases?
subQuestions.description: Measures whether long projects are broken into realistic phases with meaningful checkpoints and outputs.
subQuestions.rationale: Task decomposition is already one of the clearest strengths of modern agentic systems when goals are concrete and scoped.

proofItems.title: Benchmark suite: Long-project decomposition quality
proofItems.shortExplanation: Planning benchmarks show strong phase decomposition and milestone sequencing on well-specified multi-step goals.

subQuestions.title: Can it maintain task state over long horizons?
subQuestions.description: Tests whether the system preserves requirements, prior decisions, and unfinished work across extended workflows.
subQuestions.rationale: Long-horizon state retention is still brittle, especially once context becomes large and tasks branch.

proofItems.title: Longitudinal trial: State retention across extended workflows
proofItems.shortExplanation: Long-run trials continue to show requirement drift and forgotten commitments over extended task horizons.

subQuestions.title: Can it track dependencies and intermediate outputs?
subQuestions.description: Measures whether prerequisite work is respected and intermediate deliverables are handled in the right order.
subQuestions.rationale: Dependency tracking works on cleaner plans, but still misses hidden coupling and delayed prerequisites in larger workflows.

proofItems.title: Deployment audit: Dependency tracking in multi-step operations
proofItems.shortExplanation: Operational audits show better dependency handling than earlier systems, but still document frequent misses on cross-workstream prerequisites.

subQuestions.title: Can it re-plan after failure or changing conditions?
subQuestions.description: Tests whether the system updates its plan sensibly after delays, failed steps, or changing requirements.
subQuestions.rationale: Re-planning is often useful, but models still over-anchor on earlier structure when the task needs a deeper reset.

proofItems.title: Benchmark suite: Adaptive re-planning after workflow failure
proofItems.shortExplanation: Dynamic planning evaluations show strong recovery in moderate cases, but weaker restructuring once multiple assumptions break at once.

subQuestions.title: Can it deliver end results with limited supervision?
subQuestions.description: Measures whether the system can finish extended work with milestone-level oversight instead of dense step-by-step rescue.
subQuestions.rationale: Autonomous completion still drops sharply on real long-horizon work once priorities shift or errors compound.

proofItems.title: Deployment audit: Minimal-supervision project delivery
proofItems.shortExplanation: Field audits show some partial autonomy, but still document frequent human rescue on longer projects with moving requirements.
